jobs.title: Berater (d/m/w) Wealth Management - Mitarbeiterbank
jobs.description:
Job Description:

Teambeschreibung und Rollendetails

Wir sind ein motiviertes und engagiertes Team mit einem großen Erfahrungsschatz und einer ausgeprägten Kompetenz in sämtlichen (Wertpapier-)Produkten und Dienstleistungen in Wealth Management

Sie passen zu uns, wenn Ihnen die bedarfsgerechte Beratung von Kunden und steter Wissensaufbau zum Produktportfolio genauso viel Freude bereitet wie uns.

Sie haben bei uns die Möglichkeit auf Work at Home mit bis zu 40%.

Aufgabenbeschreibung

  • Betreut als erfahrener Relationship-Manager (d/m/w) ein eigenes Kundenportfolio im Segment WM

  • Lebt den ganzheitlichen Beratungs- und Betreuungsansatz

  • Nutzt erfolgreich bestehende Kundenbeziehungen zur Ansprache auf Empfehlung und Gewinnung von Familienangehörigen und Neukunden und ist im Markt gut vernetzt

  • Erschließt den Kunden die gesamte Produkt- und Dienstleistungspalette im Wealth Management und zieht situativ Spezialisten hinzu, um die weiteren, komplexen Bedarfe seiner Kunden umfassend zu erfüllen

  • Hält die Anforderungen an die Qualität im Wertpapiergeschäft mit den dazugehörigen und notwendigen regulatorischen Anfordernissen und Genehmigungen in der digitalen/remote Beratung ein

Fähigkeiten und Erfahrungen

  • Sie treten in der Beratung souverän, routiniert, kompetent und freundlich auf

  • Sie optimieren die Kundenzufriedenheit mit dem Ziel der langfristigen Kundenbindung

  • Ihre Kernkompetenz ist die Vermögensanlageberatung unter Einsatz der digitalen Kommunikationswege (v.a. Videotelefonie und Telefon) im Sinne des ganzheitlichen Ansatzes

  • Sie haben ausgeprägte verkäuferische Fähigkeiten und eine hohe Kundenorientierung

  • Sie sind routiniert im Umgang mit sensiblen Daten und gehen mit der nötigen Diskretion und Fingerspitzengefühl vor, ohne dabei regulatorische Anforderungen zu vergessen.

  • Gute Ausdrucksfähigkeit und ein verbindliches Auftreten runden Ihr Profil ab, Englischkenntnisse sind ein Plus

Was wir Ihnen bieten

Wir bieten eine breite Palette von Leistungen, die all Ihre beruflichen und persönlichen Bedürfnisse abdecken.

  • Emotional ausgeglichen
    Eine positive Haltung hilft uns, die Herausforderungen des Alltags zu meistern – beruflich wie privat. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beratung in schwierigen Lebenssituationen und Angeboten zur Förderung mentaler Gesundheit.

  • Körperlich fit
    Mit Angeboten zur Aufrechterhaltung Ihrer persönlichen Gesundheit und einem förderlichen beruflichen Umfeld hilft Ihnen die Bank, körperlich fit zu bleiben. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie umfangreichen Check-up Untersuchungen, Impfangeboten und Beratung zur gesunden Lebensführung.

  • Sozial vernetzt
    Der Austausch mit anderen eröffnet uns neue Perspektiven, bringt uns beruflich wie persönlich voran und stärkt unser Selbstvertrauen und Wohlbefinden. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Unterstützung durch den pme Familienservice, das FitnessCenter Job, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle (bspw. Teilzeit, Jobtandem, hybrides Arbeiten) sowie einer umfangreichen Kultur der Vielfalt, Chancengleichheit und Teilhabe.

  • Finanziell abgesichert
    Die Bank sichert Sie nicht nur während Ihrer aktiven Karriere, sondern auch für die Zukunft finanziell ab und unterstützt Ihre Flexibilität sowie Mobilität – egal ob privat oder beruflich. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beitragsplänen für Altersvorsorge, Bankdienstleistungen für Mitarbeiter*innen, Firmenfahrrad oder dem Deutschlandticket.

Da die Benefits je nach Standort geringfügig variieren, gehen Sie bitte bei konkreten Fragen auf Ihre Recruiter / Ihre Recruiterin zu.

Die Stelle wird in Voll- und in Teilzeit angeboten.

Bei Fragen zum Rekrutierungsprozess steht Ihnen Esther Grandmann gerne zur Verfügung.

Kontakt Esther Grandmann: +49 69-91045 443

jobs.title: Head of Applied AI Engineering – Investment Banking - Director
jobs.description:
Engineer the future of global finance. At Citi, our Tech team doesn’t just support finance – we are helping to redefine it. Every day, $5 trillion crosses through our network. We do business in 180+ countries operating at a scale few can match. From deploying advanced AI to helping shape global markets, we build systems that matter. Look to join a team where your work helps influence economies, your ideas can drive innovation and outcomes, and your growth is backed by mentorship, continuous learning and flexibility with potential hybrid work opportunities. Help solve real-world challenges that touch millions and get the opportunity to build the future of finance with Citi Tech.

Role Summary
We are seeking a visionary and hands-on Head of Applied AI Engineering to lead the design, development, and deployment of AI-driven solutions for our Banking division, covering Investment (ECM, DCM, M&A), Corporate and Commercial Banking. This role will bridge the front office and advanced technology, bringing intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making into the heart of dealmaking.


The ideal candidate will combine deep AI engineering expertise with a strong understanding of the investment banking ecosystem, business workflows, and secure enterprise-scale deployment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategic AI Leadership - Partner with senior bankers and business leads to identify high-impact AI opportunities across deal origination, client intelligence, market analysis, and pitch automation. Develop and execute the AI engineering roadmap aligned to Banking tech strategy and enterprise architecture.
  • AI Engineering & Architecture - Lead the design and development of scalable AI systems using LLMs, NLP, knowledge graphs, and machine learning pipelines. Architect secure, compliant AI solutions that integrate with market data, CRM, internal knowledge bases, and document repositories.
  • Data Strategy - Drive integration of structured (e.g., financial data, CRM) and unstructured (e.g., filings, call transcripts, news) data to enable advanced insights. Oversee data engineering and ML feature pipelines in collaboration with data teams.
  • Productization & Delivery - Convert proofs-of-concept into scalable, enterprise-grade tools. Embed AI solutions into banker workflows via co-pilots, dashboards, and smart document assistants.
  • Governance & Compliance - Ensure all AI systems meet internal standards for explain ability, fairness, and compliance with regulatory obligations. Collaborate with risk, legal, and compliance teams on AI model governance.
  • Team Building & Leadership - Build and lead a high-performing team of AI engineers, ML specialists, and applied data scientists. Foster a culture of innovation, delivery excellence, and business alignment.
     

Qualifications
Must-Have Skills & Experience

  • Significant proven data science / AI engineering experience, with multiple years leading teams in financial services or enterprise environments.
  • Demonstrated success building AI applications in investment banking, asset management, or capital markets domains.
  • Deep expertise in ML, NLP, LLMs, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), embeddings, and modern MLOps practices.
  • Strong experience working with both structured financial datasets and unstructured data sources (e.g., filings, call transcripts, research).
  • Familiarity with front-office workflows in ECM, DCM, M&A, and investment research.
  • Experience deploying AI solutions in secure, high-compliance environments (on-premise, hybrid cloud, or private cloud).
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, especially with senior bankers and C-level executives.


Preferred

  • Experience with knowledge graphs and graph-based search.
  • Familiarity with tools like Bloomberg, Refinitiv, Capital IQ, FactSet, PitchBook.
  • Prior work on AI co-pilots, document summarization tools, or automated pitch generation.
  • Exposure to enterprise CRM systems and client intelligence platforms.
  • Advanced degree in Computer Science, AI, Applied Mathematics, or related quantitative field.


What Success Looks Like

  • AI tools embedded into daily workflows of bankers and analysts.
  • Reduction in manual effort across client targeting, pitch preparation, and market monitoring.
  • Data assets and ML models fully aligned with enterprise governance and architecture.
  • Scalable AI platform that evolves with the pace of the business and innovation.


Why Join Us?
Shape the future of investment banking with cutting-edge AI.
Work at the intersection of technology, capital markets, and innovation.
High visibility and impact across the front office and C-suite.
Access to world-class data, partners, and AI infrastructure.

What we’ll provide you

By joining Citi London, you will not only be part of a business casual workplace with a hybrid working model (up to 2 days working at home per week), but also receive a competitive base salary (which is annually reviewed), and enjoy a whole host of additional benefits such as:

  • 27 days annual leave (plus bank holidays)
  • A discretional annual performance related bonus
  • Private Medical Care & Life Insurance
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Pension Plan
  • Paid Parental Leave
  • Special discounts for employees, family, and friends
  • Access to an array of learning and development resources

Alongside these benefits Citi is committed to ensuring our workplace is where everyone feels comfortable coming to work as their whole self, every day. We want the best talent around the world to be energized to join us, motivated to stay and empowered to thrive.

jobs.title: Banking, Investment Banking, Full Time Analyst, Madrid, Spain 2026
jobs.description:
Citi’s Banking is seeking Investment Banking Analysts in Madrid who plays a crucial role in supporting clients through complex financial endeavours. This position is primarily responsible for assisting clients in their efforts to raise capital within the financial markets. Additionally, the Investment Banking Analyst provides strategic advisory services for a wide range of financial transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and other corporate finance activities, working in close coordination with the Banking team. The Analyst also serves as an important intermediary in trading operations for clients. The overarching objective of this role is to contribute to the formulation of sophisticated investment strategies and facilitate capital raising, thereby acting as a key strategic advisor to our valued clients.

Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate closely with senior Corporate & Investment Bank (CIB) officers and seasoned professionals, actively participating in client transactions, projects, and assignments as a vital junior member of the Investment Banking team.

  • Assume responsibility for meticulously organizing and analysing complex financial data, which forms the foundation for client proposals and strategic plans.

  • Prepare compelling written recommendations, comprehensive valuation materials, and insightful statistical exhibits for both pitch presentations and active deals.

  • Conduct thorough economic and financial research, providing critical insights to support deal execution and client advisory.

  • Systematically evaluate a company's financial performance, benchmarking it against overall industry trends and broader market dynamics to identify key insights and opportunities.

  • Construct and maintain complex financial models, essential for accurate valuation purposes and strategic decision-making.

  • Proactively assess and mitigate risk in all business decisions, demonstrating unwavering consideration for the firm's reputation and diligently safeguarding Citigroup, its clients, and assets. This includes ensuring strict compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies, exercising sound ethical judgment in all personal and professional conduct, and transparently escalating, managing, and reporting any control issues.

  • Consistently demonstrates clear, concise, and highly effective written and verbal communication skills, crucial for internal and external interactions.

  • Proven ability to perform exceptionally well under pressure in a fast-paced and rapidly changing environment, consistently meeting stringent deadlines.

Qualifications:

  • 2-5 years of relevant experience within the investment banking sector or a closely related financial field.

  • Predicted or obtained a UK 2:1 (or equivalent academic achievement) in a Bachelor's / University Degree

  • Fluency in English and Spanish is essential, encompassing both written and verbal proficiency.

Education:

  • A Bachelor's / University degree in Finance or closely related areas of Business Administration is required.

jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst, Technology M&A
jobs.description:
The Investment Banking Analyst is an intermediate level position responsible for assisting clients in raising funds in the capital markets, as well as in providing strategic advisory services for mergers, acquisitions and other types of financial transactions in coordination with the Institutional Banking team. The Investment Banking Analyst also serves as an intermediary in trading for clients. The overall objective of this role is to act as a strategic advisor to our clients by formulating investment strategies and raising capital for clients.

Responsibilities:

  • Work with senior Corporate & Investment Bank (CIB) officers and professionals and participate in particular client transactions, projects or assignments as the junior member of the Investment Banking team
  • Responsible for organizing and analyzing sophisticated financial data used in client proposals and strategic plans
  • Prepare written recommendations, valuation materials and statistical exhibits for pitches and live deals
  • Conduct economic/financial research
  • Evaluate company’s financial performance compared to overall industry and market trends
  • Build complex financial models for valuation purposes
  • Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.

Qualifications:

  • 2-5 years of relevant experience
  • Bachelor's degree in Finance or closely related areas of Business Administration
  • Series 79 and 63 licenses
  • Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to work under pressure in a rapidly changing environment in order to meet deadlines

Education:

  • Bachelor's degree/University degree or equivalent experience

This job description provides a high-level review of the types of work performed. Other job-related duties may be assigned as required.

jobs.title: Associate, Capital Solutions - Private Wealth Client Coverage
jobs.description:
About General Atlantic

General Atlantic is a leading global investor with more than four and a half decades of experience providing capital and strategic support for over 830 companies throughout its history. Established in 1980, General Atlantic continues to be a dedicated partner to visionary founders and investors seeking to build dynamic businesses and create long term value. Guided by the conviction that entrepreneurs can be incredible agents of transformational change, the firm combines a collaborative global approach, sector specific expertise, a long-term investment horizon, and a deep understanding of growth drivers to partner with and scale innovative businesses around the world. The firm leverages its patient capital, operational expertise, and global platform to support a diversified investment platform spanning Growth Equity, Credit, Climate, and Sustainable Infrastructure strategies. General Atlantic manages approximately $123 billion in assets under management, inclusive of all strategies, as of December 31, 2025, with more than 900 professionals in 20 countries across five regions. For more information on General Atlantic, please visit: www.generalatlantic.com.

Position Summary

General Atlantic’s Capital Solutions team is focused on marketing the firm’s global investment strategies to institutional and private wealth investors worldwide. The Capital Solutions team manages all aspects of client coverage and the fundraising process by building relationships with both current and prospective investors. 

General Atlantic is looking for a high-caliber Associate to join its Private Wealth Capital Solutions team based in New York to support financial advisor client coverage across an assigned U.S. region.  The Client Coverage Associate will work in close partnership with regional Client Coverage leads, covering financial advisors across both private bank and wirehouse wealth management platforms, providing proactive sales and client service support to drive fundraising and deepen relationships advisor across General Atlantic’s Growth Equity, Climate, Infrastructure and Credit strategies in their assigned region.  The candidate will work closely with senior members of General Atlantic’s global Capital Solutions team, while collaborating with professionals across the firm to design and execute scaled fundraising, financial advisor engagement, and client service initiatives across the private wealth channel.

Responsibilities

The candidate will have a diverse set of client coverage and fundraising responsibilities, including:

  • Partner with Private Wealth Client Coverage regional leads to develop new, foster, and maintain relationships with financial advisors at leading private wealth management platforms (e.g., private banks and wirehouses), supporting fundraising across General Atlantic’s product suite
  • Execute proactive outbound sales activity to both existing and prospective financial advisor allocators to educate them on General Atlantic’s strategies and drive new fundraising opportunities
  • Assist in the management of territory coverage and financial advisor sales pipeline in a highly organized and data‑driven manner, leveraging analytics to prioritize engagements, track progress, and optimize marketing efforts within assigned regions
  • Conduct timely and detailed follow‑up after advisor and client interactions, ensuring delivery of tailored materials and responses to information requests
  • Maintain accurate, high‑quality CRM records of contacts, activities, meeting notes, and pipeline as well as assist in the preparation of regular reports on financial advisor engagement, territory activity, and fundraising progress for Capital Solutions leadership
  • Develop and maintain deep product and market expertise across General Atlantic’s investment capabilities and structures (including both private funds and evergreen vehicles), while communicating complex private markets and alternative investment concepts in a clear, concise manner to financial advisors and their end clients
  • Effectively coordinate across internal teams and wealth platform partners to help organize roadshows, webinars, conferences, and advisor education events; managing planning, logistics, materials, and follow‑up to maximize financial advisor participation and engagement
  • Build and sustain ongoing connectivity with financial advisors through consistent phone and email touchpoints, helping to deepen relationships, address client service inquiries, and support product update and review calls in partnership with senior client coverage

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree required; progress toward CFA and/or CAIA charters preferred
  • Minimum 2+ years of investment product sales experience working with financial advisors at leading wealth management firms (wirehouses, private banks, RIAs or independent broker-dealers), ideally with experience in private market, alternative investment strategies and products
  • Strong understanding of financial markets and investment strategies, including familiarity with private market alternative asset classes and both private and evergreen fund structures (e.g., private funds, interval funds, BDCs, REITs, evergreen structures)
  • Excellent communication skills: presentation, written and interpersonal, with the professionalism and presence to represent General Atlantic and its capabilities to financial advisors and their end clients
  • Strong organizational skills, high attention to detail and strong work ethic
  • Highly motivated with ability to work independently, set priorities and meet deadline and fundraising goals
  • Ability to work in a team-oriented sales environment with a collaborative and transparent mindset

GA Value

  • Competitive compensation
  • Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance plans
  • Retirement savings plan with employer matching
  • Professional development opportunities and ongoing training
  • Collaborative and inclusive work culture with opportunities for advancement

General Atlantic offers a robust reward program to all employees that will support you and your family, maintaining fulfilling, secure and healthy lives now and into the future, which includes but is not limited to medical insurance, retirement savings contributions, mental and physical health resources and an equal pay program that allows you to earn a base salary of $135,000 – 155,000of which the exact amount offered will be driven by the degree to which you meet role requirements, additional special skills and experience.  Additional reward programs, such as annual discretionary bonuses and long-term incentive programs, are available for eligible employees and are offered as recognition for performance and one’s contributions towards the organization’s success.

General Atlantic is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical​​​ condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or ​expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

By completing this application, you are consenting to General Atlantic’s processing and use of your personal data. Information on how General Atlantic will use and process your data can be found here: https://www.generalatlantic.com/privacy-notice/

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Private Wealth Product Development and Management
jobs.description:
About General Atlantic

General Atlantic is a leading global investor with more than four and a half decades of experience providing capital and strategic support for over 830 companies throughout its history. Established in 1980, General Atlantic continues to be a dedicated partner to visionary founders and investors seeking to build dynamic businesses and create long term value. Guided by the conviction that entrepreneurs can be incredible agents of transformational change, the firm combines a collaborative global approach, sector specific expertise, a long-term investment horizon, and a deep understanding of growth drivers to partner with and scale innovative businesses around the world. The firm leverages its patient capital, operational expertise, and global platform to support a diversified investment platform spanning Growth Equity, Credit, Climate, and Sustainable Infrastructure strategies. General Atlantic manages approximately $123 billion in assets under management, inclusive of all strategies, as of December 31, 2025, with more than 900 professionals in 20 countries across five regions. For more information on General Atlantic, please visit: www.generalatlantic.com.

Position Summary

General Atlantic’s Capital Solutions team is focused on marketing the firm’s global investment strategies to institutional and private wealth investors worldwide. The Capital Solutions team manages all aspects of client coverage and the fundraising process by building relationships with both current and prospective investors.  Within the Private Wealth Capital Solutions team, the Private Wealth Product Development & Management function is responsible for designing, structuring, launching, and managing new product solutions primarily tailored for private wealth channel distribution, including via private banks, wirehouses, RIAs, and other intermediary platforms.

General Atlantic is seeking a high-caliber Associate or Senior Associate to join its Private Wealth Product Development & Management team in New York. This role will support the full product lifecycle for General Atlantic’s private wealth offerings, including strategy and fund product design, competitor product analysis and reporting, ongoing product management, and client-facing reporting.  The role will work closely with Capital Solutions senior leadership as well as colleagues across General Atlantic’s private wealth client coverage, investment teams, finance, legal and compliance, operations, and marketing to help develop and deliver institutional-quality products, marketing materials, and analytics to private wealth investors.

Responsibilities

The candidate will have a diverse set of product development and management responsibilities, including:

  • Supporting product strategy, design, and structuring for private wealth channel delivery by working across General Atlantic’s organization to help design evergreen product vehicles and terms based on strategy, target client base, wealth platform requirements, and competitive intelligence
  • Helping coordinate and manage cross-functional product working groups – including product, sales, operations, tax, legal and compliance, finance, client services, technology, and marketing – to drive end-to-end product development, manage workstreams and timelines, and ensure smooth execution of product launches and post-launch processes
  • Prepare and maintain marketing materials and content for client delivery, including fund marketing materials, portfolio and performance updates as well as market and strategy commentary tailored to private wealth audiences
  • Analyze market intelligence and competitor offerings to assist in the identification of opportunities for new wealth product development, helping shape private wealth product strategy and execution
  • Collect and maintain competitive intelligence on fundraising, product structures and terms, and market positioning, while synthesizing information into clear recommendations on product design, execution, and differentiation
  • Develop and maintain high-quality product marketing and due diligence materials, including pitchbooks, product profiles, fact sheets, case studies, portfolio updates, and platform-specific content for private banks, wirehouses, RIAs, and other wealth platform intermediaries
  • Support wealth platform due diligence processes by serving as a key coordinator of RFPs, DDQs, RFIs, and related questionnaires as well as ad hoc information requests, and by liaising with colleagues across General Atlantic’s functional groups
  • Contribute to ongoing product management, governance, and reporting by supporting recurring and ad hoc product communications, helping ensure alignment with regulatory and platform requirements, and assisting in the development of thought leadership and marketing content that showcases General Atlantic’s strategies and market views

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree required; progress toward CFA and/or CAIA charters preferred
  • 3–6+ years of relevant experience in private market investment product management, wealth product development; experience at an alternative asset manager and/or private wealth alts platform is strongly preferred
  • Strong working knowledge of private markets and alternative investment strategies, including familiarity with private equity, infrastructure and/or private credit, and fund vehicle structures (e.g., closed-end drawdown funds, evergreen/registered structures, interval funds, BDCs, REITs, feeders/access funds)
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex investment and structural concepts into clear, concise, investor-ready materials
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills, including a strong skill set working with performance data, portfolio analytics, and large data sets in Excel
  • Demonstrated project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines in a dynamic, fast-paced environment
  • High attention to detail and a proactive, ownership-oriented mindset
  • Proven ability to build relationships and work collaboratively across sales, investment, finance, legal and compliance, operations, and marketing, as well as with external partners
  • Advanced skills in PowerPoint, Excel, and Word; experience with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and data room platforms (e.g., Intralinks, Datasite) is a plus
  • Ability to work in a team-oriented environment with a collaborative and transparent mindset

GA Value

  • Competitive compensation
  • Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance plans
  • Retirement savings plan with employer matching
  • Professional development opportunities and ongoing training
  • Collaborative and inclusive work culture with opportunities for advancement

General Atlantic offers a robust reward program to all employees that will support you and your family, maintaining fulfilling, secure and healthy lives now and into the future, which includes but is not limited to medical insurance, retirement savings contributions, mental and physical health resources and an equal pay program that allows you to earn a base salary of $135,000 – 190,000 of which the exact amount offered will be driven by the degree to which you meet role requirements, additional special skills and experience.  Additional reward programs, such as annual discretionary bonuses and long-term incentive programs, are available for eligible employees and are offered as recognition for performance and one’s contributions towards the organization’s success.

General Atlantic is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical​​​ condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or ​expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

By completing this application, you are consenting to General Atlantic’s processing and use of your personal data. Information on how General Atlantic will use and process your data can be found here: https://www.generalatlantic.com/privacy-notice/

jobs.title: Associate
jobs.description:
Company Overview

We are the better way to work in finance. As private equity’s value creation partner, we sit at the heart of PE—where sponsors and CFOs meet. Through financial consulting rooted in data, technology, and AI, we help clients drive value—supporting the office of the CFO to drive end-to-end value creation.

If you crave challenging work and are looking to grow, come solve complex issues alongside 1,400+ finance & technology experts in a supportive, collaborative environment.

Backed by premier private equity firms and headquartered in New York with 10 offices around the globe, we are a high-growth, entrepreneurial firm looking for people who want to be part of building something great. Come make your mark. 

Turnaround & Restructuring

Our nationally recognized turnaround, restructuring and advisory team serves companies and their stakeholders across a wide spectrum of industries and sizes, with a focus on the middle market. We provide clients with a team of seasoned professionals who have notable track records of creating value through both operational turnarounds and financial restructurings.

We are actively recruiting Turnaround & Restructuring professionals to join our team. You will provide extensive financial and operational support on client engagements across a variety of industries and markets; utilize experience and on the job training to successfully deliver reports, models, work product and advice that helps guide the direction and decisions related to client turnaround and/or restructuring objectives.

This role can be based in any of our US office locations and is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work remotely 2 days a week. Ideal candidates should be local to the desired location..

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

What You’ll Do:

  • Perform analysis of current and historical business performance and capital structure
  • Develop 13-week cash flow forecasts that outline the liquidity profile and cash needs
  • Create dynamic financial models that exhibit the client’s historical and potential future performance
  • Support the creation and preparation of corporate strategic plans
  • Provide implementation support to approved business plans and strategies
  • Engage with client personnel and management as necessary to achieve objectives
  • Develop and furnish appraisal of business options and contingency plans as needed
  • Effectively gather, analyze, and organize large data sets which may be incomplete
  • Support the development of quality client deliverables
  • Provide interim support on operating functions and job duties as directed
  • Assist in bankruptcy preparation and administration
  • Travel to client site as needed

You Have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance and/or accounting is preferred
  • Graduate business degree with concentration in finance, accounting and/or operations preferred
  • Minimum 3+ years of relevant professional work experience
  • Hands-on experience building / developing / maintaining fully dynamic, integrated 3-statement financial, and 13-week cash flow models
  • Highly proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to build and sustain strong and trusted relationships with colleagues and stakeholders
  • Demonstrated expertise and aptitude with conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses, particularly with constructing integrated financial models, analyzing financial underperformance and related services
  • Experience working on projects delivering independent business reviews, short term cash flow assessments, capital structure analysis, and contingency planning
  • Capacity to thrive in a fast-paced, challenging, and uncertain environment
  • Deep understanding of how to interpret and analyze financial statements
  • Possess strong analytical and business writing skills
  • Able to work well under pressure and independently yet understand when to ask for guidance

You Are:

  • A self-starter with a strong work ethic
  • A leader of others; you lead by example
  • A strong team player, able to work with team members across all levels
  • Able to effectively communicate complex issues and solutions, and raise issues to senior team members when necessary
  • Comfortable managing projects with multiple complex workstreams while also focused on your single workstream (you are managing and doing).
  • Excited to be part of a growing team, with a focus on driving future growth
  • Full of entrepreneurial spirit and comfortable in a fluid, flat organization
  • Knowledgeable about the relationship between Private Equity sponsors and their portfolio companies
  • Passionate about delivering exceptional client service
  • Someone who enjoys mentoring others and doing meaningful work
  • Willing to get your hands dirty in the details of a project while simultaneously seeing the whole picture

The annual salary for this role ranges from: $97,750 to $150,000 USD + benefits + bonus. Actual compensation packages are determined by evaluating a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to geographic location, skill set, years and depth of experience, education, certifications, cost of labor and internal equity.

Accordion is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills.  We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical​​​ condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or ​expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

Please note that as per Accordion policy, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters unless such recruiters are engaged to provide candidates for a specified opening and in alignment with our inclusive diversity values. Any employment agency, person or entity that submits an unsolicited resume does so with the understanding that Accordion will have the right to hire that applicant at its discretion without any fee owed to the submitting employment agency, person or entity.

jobs.title: Corporate Development Manager
jobs.description:
Company Overview

We are the better way to work in finance. As private equity’s value creation partner, we sit at the heart of PE—where sponsors and CFOs meet. Through financial consulting rooted in data, technology, and AI, we help clients drive value—supporting the office of the CFO to drive end-to-end value creation.

If you crave challenging work and are looking to grow, come solve complex issues alongside 1,400+ finance & technology experts in a supportive, collaborative environment. 

Backed by premier private equity firms and headquartered in New York with 10 offices around the globe, we are a high-growth, entrepreneurial firm looking for people who want to be part of building something great. Come make your mark. 

Position Overview 

The Corporate Development Manager will own end-to-end inorganic growth initiatives — from sourcing and evaluating opportunities to negotiating terms and assisting with post-close integration — to advance our strategic roadmap and value creation objectives. This new hire will partner with the Head of M&A to:

  • Drive the full M&A lifecycle (build pipeline, diligence, valuation, deal terms, close, and integrate)
  • Translate investment theses into actionable business cases with quantified synergies, return profiles, and execution plans 

This role will be based in our NYC office and is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work remotely 2 days a week, blending the best of both worlds for efficiency and collaboration. Ideal candidates should be local to the desired location (NYC).

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

What You’ll Do:

  • Pipeline and Sourcing – Build and maintain a prioritized funnel of targets aligned to strategic themes; cultivate relationships with founders, bankers, and investors; triage inbound opportunities with crisp fit/no-fit rationale
  • Deal Evaluation – Lead commercial, financial, and operational assessments; structure scenarios, sensitivities, and synergy cases; summarize go/no-go recommendations for executive stakeholders
  • Financial Modeling and Valuation – Build robust three-statement, DCF, trading/transaction comps, and LBO-style downside cases; frame offer ranges and walk-away points; align structure with risk-adjusted returns
  • Diligence Leadership – Run cross-functional diligence workstreams (legal, finance, tax, HR, operations); own the data room, Q&A tracking, red flag resolution, and IC-ready findings
  • Deal Structuring and Negotiation – Draft and iterate key documents (NDAs, LOIs, term sheets, purchase agreements)
  • Integration Planning and Oversight – Serve as DRI for Day-1/100, value-capture workplans, and KPI tracking; ensure explicit owners, timelines, and interlocks across functions; course-correct against synergy drift
  • Executive Communications and Governance – Prepare investment memos, IC materials, board updates, and post-close readouts; establish deal KPIs and cadence for performance management
  • Market and Competitive Intelligence – Maintain a live view of sector dynamics, adjacencies, and disruptors; refresh build/partner/buy perspectives and target lists accordingly

You Have:

  • Minimum 3–6 years in corporate development, investment banking, and/or private equity with demonstrated end-to-end exposure to M&A processes (modeling, diligence, negotiation, and closing)
  • Expert proficiency in deal execution and company evaluation; strong command of transaction structures
  • Strong document familiarity (NDAs, LOIs, SPAs), term mechanics (earnouts, RWI, escrows), and data room best practices
  • High-impact communication skills (concise memos, IC/board materials, stakeholder alignment) and executive presence
  • Industry competence in tech-enabled services, professional services, or human capital services preferred 

You Are:

  • Excited to be part of a growing team, with a focus on driving future growth
  • Passionate about delivering exceptional client service
  • Knowledgeable about the relationship between Private Equity sponsors and their portfolio companies, particularly within the office of the CFO
  • Willing to get your hands dirty in the details of a project while simultaneously seeing the whole picture
  • Comfortable managing projects with multiple complex workstreams while also focused on your single workstream (you are managing and doing)
  • A self-starter with a strong work ethic
  • Full of entrepreneurial spirit and comfortable in a fluid, flat organization
  • Able to effectively communicate complex issues and solutions, and raise issues to senior team members when necessary
  • A team player, able to work with team members across all levels
  • Someone who enjoys mentoring others and doing meaningful work
  • A leader of others; you lead by example

The annual salary for this role ranges from: $160,000 to $200,000 USD + benefits + bonus. Actual compensation packages are determined by evaluating a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to geographic location, skill set, years and depth of experience, education, certifications, cost of labor and internal equity.

Accordion is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills.  We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

Please note that as per Accordion policy, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters unless such recruiters are engaged to provide candidates for a specified opening and in alignment with our inclusive diversity values. Any employment agency, person or entity that submits an unsolicited resume does so with the understanding that Accordion will have the right to hire that applicant at its discretion without any fee owed to the submitting employment agency, person or entity.

You Have:

  • Ability to develop the framework and take the lead on preparing reports

You Are:

  • Passionate about delivering exceptional client service
  • Able to build credibility and trust with the client
  • Knowledgeable about the relationship between Private Equity sponsors and their portfolio companies, particularly within the office of the CFO
  • Willing to get your hands dirty in the details of a project while simultaneously seeing the whole picture
  • Comfortable managing projects with multiple complex workstreams while also focused on your single workstream (you are managing and doing)
  • A self-starter with a strong work ethic
  • Full of entrepreneurial spirit and comfortable in a fluid, flat organization
  • Able to effectively communicate complex issues and solutions, and raise issues to senior team members when necessary

  • A strong team player, able to work with team members across all levels
  • Someone who enjoys mentoring others and doing meaningful work

The annual salary for this role ranges from: $178,500 to $250,000 USD + benefits + bonus. jobs.title: Vice President
jobs.description:
Company Overview

We are the better way to work in finance. As private equity’s value creation partner, we sit at the heart of PE—where sponsors and CFOs meet. Through financial consulting rooted in data, technology, and AI, we help clients drive value—supporting the office of the CFO to drive end-to-end value creation.

If you crave challenging work and are looking to grow, come solve complex issues alongside 1,400+ finance & technology experts in a supportive, collaborative environment.

Backed by premier private equity firms and headquartered in New York with 10 offices around the globe, we are a high-growth, entrepreneurial firm looking for people who want to be part of building something great. Come make your mark. 

Turnaround & Restructuring

Our nationally recognized turnaround, restructuring and advisory team serves companies and their stakeholders across a wide spectrum of industries and sizes, with a focus on the middle market. We provide clients with a team of seasoned professionals who have notable track records of creating value through both operational turnarounds and financial restructurings.

We are actively recruiting Turnaround & Restructuring professionals to join our team. You will provide extensive financial and operational support on client engagements across a variety of industries and markets; utilize experience and on the job training to successfully deliver reports, models, and work product that helps guide the direction and decisions related to client turnaround and/or restructuring objectives.

This role can be based out of our Chicago, Dallas, or New York office and is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work remotely 2 days a week. Ideal candidates should be local to the desired location.

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

What You’ll Do:

  • Perform analysis of current and historical business performance and capital structure
  • Develop 13-week cash flow forecasts that outline the liquidity profile and cash needs

  • Support the development of quality client deliverables
  • Provide interim support on operating functions and job duties as directed
  • Assist in bankruptcy preparation and administration
  • Travel to client site as needed

You Have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance and/or Accounting is preferred
  • Graduate business degree with concentration in finance, accounting and/or operations preferred
  • Minimum 6+ years of relevant professional work experience
  • Hands-on experience building / developing / maintaining fully dynamic, integrated 3-statement financial, and 13-week cash flow models

  • Ability to build and sustain strong and trusted relationships with colleagues and stakeholders
  • Demonstrated expertise and aptitude with conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses, particularly with constructing integrated financial models, analyzing financial underperformance and related services

  • Capacity to thrive in a fast-paced, challenging, and uncertain environment
  • Deep understanding of how to interpret and analyze financial statements
  • Strong analytical and business writing skills
  • Ability to work well under pressure and independently yet understand when to ask for guidance

You Are:

  • A self-starter with a strong work ethic
  • A leader of others; you lead by example
  • A strong team player, able to work with team members across all levels
  • Able to effectively communicate complex issues and solutions, and raise issues to senior team members when necessary
  • Comfortable managing projects with multiple complex workstreams while also focused on your single workstream (you are managing and doing).

jobs.title: Investment Banking Associate: Gaming
jobs.description:
Investment Banking Associate

at GLC Advisors & Co., LLC (View all jobs)

Firm Overview

GLC Advisors & Co. is a leading independent advisory firm providing objective, conflict-free advice on complex strategic and financial transactions. The firm operates under a senior-led execution model, partnering closely with boards of directors, management teams, and financial sponsors on critical corporate decisions.

GLC’s partners and senior bankers collectively have advised on 900+ completed transactions totaling more than $800 billion in aggregate transaction value, with prior experience at the world’s most selective bulge-bracket and elite boutique investment banks. The firm is consistently engaged on high-impact, analytically intensive mandates across mergers and acquisitions, capital structure advisory, restructurings, and valuation matters.

GLC Advisors maintains offices in New York, Denver, Los Angeles, and San Francisco.

Role Overview 

GLC Advisors is seeking a top-tier Investment Banking Associate to join its Gaming advisory franchise in Denver. This position is intended for candidates with exceptional technical ability, sound judgment, and a demonstrated interest in executing complex transactions in a lean, performance-driven environment.

The Gaming practice advises across the full interactive entertainment and gaming ecosystem, including gaming technology and software, payments and infrastructure, land-based gaming and entertainment, iGaming, sports betting, and lodging. The team regularly serves as a trusted advisor to industry leaders, growth-oriented companies, and sophisticated financial sponsors on high-profile strategic and financial transactions.

Associates are expected to function as core execution leaders on live mandates, working directly with senior bankers and clients. The role offers substantial responsibility, early client exposure, and meaningful influence over transaction outcomes.

Responsibilities

Associates will assume significant ownership across all phases of transaction execution, including:

  • Leading the analytical and execution workstreams for M&A, financing, and restructuring assignments
  • Working directly with clients, counterparties, and legal and accounting advisors to advance transactions from mandate through closing
  • Overseeing and mentoring Analysts while ensuring the highest standards of analytical rigor and presentation quality
  • Developing detailed financial models, valuation analyses, and transaction materials used in board-level and investor discussions
  • Conducting in-depth industry, company, and strategic analyses to support advisory recommendations
  • Participating in management presentations, due diligence processes, and negotiation discussions

Qualifications

GLC Advisors seeks candidates with a demonstrated record of excellence, including:

  • Outstanding analytical capability, intellectual curiosity, and professional judgment
  • Prior investment banking experience as an Analyst or Associate strongly preferred
  • 2–4 years of relevant transaction experience in M&A, capital markets, or restructuring
  • Advanced financial modeling, valuation, and accounting proficiency
  • Ability to independently manage complex workstreams in a fast-paced, high-expectation environment
  • Strong leadership skills and experience managing junior team members
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • High level of attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • FINRA Series 79 or Series 7 strongly preferred (required within 90 days of start date)

Compensation & Culture

GLC Advisors offers highly competitive compensation, including performance-based bonus opportunities, commensurate with experience and contribution. The firm provides a comprehensive benefits package and fosters a collegial, intellectually rigorous, and entrepreneurial culture, with a strong emphasis on accountability, professional development, and excellence in execution.

Equal Opportunity Employer

GLC Advisors is an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable law. The firm complies with all requirements related to reasonable accommodation.

jobs.title: Investment Banking Intern
jobs.description:
SUMMER INTERN, INVESTMENT BANKING

COMPANY: GLC Advisors & Co., LLC

LOCATION: Denver, CO

POSITION TITLE: Investment Banking Summer Intern 

EMPLOYMENT TYPE: Internship

PREFERRED START DATE: June 2026

FIRM OVERVIEW:

GLC is a leading independent investment banking firm that delivers objective, senior- level advice within a number of industries including: business services, consumer & retail, energy & power, financial services, gaming, general industrials, healthcare, media & telecom, metals & mining and technology.

GLC was founded in 2009 and brings financial advisory experience from both Wall Street and specialized boutique investment banking firms. With over 60 professionals, we have worked through multiple economic cycles dating back to the 1980’s and have led more than 900 transactions representing over $800 billion in total transaction value.

Our comprehensive service offerings involve all aspects of sell-side and buy-side M&A, capital raising and restructuring.

Our offices are in New York, Denver, Los Angeles and San Francisco. For more information visit www.glca.com.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Individuals will be assigned a wide variety of projects and given as much responsibility as their experience and capabilities permit. Responsibilities include:

  • Building financial models and performing valuation analyses
  • Preparing presentations, company offering memorandums, management presentations, and other marketing materials
  • Analyzing company historical financial statements and prospective financial information
  • Conducting extensive industry and company research
  • Collaborating within M&A execution teams

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Current undergraduate or graduate student with an expected graduation date in Summer 2027
  • Prior experience in corporate finance, accounting, private equity, investment banking, or related financial services
  • Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration or Management, Economics, or other degree from an accredited university or college
  • Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint, as well as preferred experience with other financial information tools such as S&P Capital IQ and PitchBook
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills with a high level of attention to detail
  • Outstanding work ethic with ability to perform in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong desire to develop modeling and financial statement analysis skills
  • Ability and willingness to work hard beyond typical work week hours

OFFERING:

We offer an exciting work environment with an entrepreneurial spirit. This position offers the opportunity to gain in-depth and diverse work experience in investment banking.

Please include in your cover letter why you are interested in our Denver team and Denver, CO more broadly.

jobs.title: Research Analyst - Europe/NATO Country Intelligence
jobs.description:
Janes empowers military, government, and defence leaders to act with confidence in an increasingly complex world. Our trusted defence, security, and geopolitical information delivered through seamless digital platforms and system integrations—turns overwhelming data into clear, actionable intelligence and insight. By filling critical information gaps, Janes helps customers analyse threats, accelerate decisions, and stay ahead of emerging challenges.

Job purpose:

The Research Analyst will support our open-source research and analysis across allocated countries in the NATO/Europe region, driving the update and enhancement of foundational country profiles covering Political, Military, Economic, Social, Information, and Infrastructure (PMESII), Chemical, Biological and Nuclear and Terrorism & Insurgency domains. The analyst will monitor, assess, and review ICD-203-aligned analysis reports covering domestic and external risks affecting national security and stability in allocated NATO/Europe countries, including terrorism and hostile states for decision-makers and stakeholders. The Research Analyst will report to the Manager of NATO/Europe.

How you will contribute at Janes:

  • Lead extensive open-source research into national security and risk factors affecting allocated NATO/Europe member states.
  • Update foundational intelligence products, ensuring they reflect current intelligence priorities and emerging threats in the region.
  • Support individual and team research projects that contribute to risk assessment products and strategic intelligence outputs.
  • Contribute to the development of analytical methodologies and best practices across the team.

The ideal skills and experience for this role are:

  • Knowledge of NATO/Europe security and geopolitical dynamics.
  • Strong OSINT collection and analysis skills, with the ability to identify and interpret key indicators
  • Excellent written and verbal communication and team integration skills
  • Strong data analysis skills and proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint, as well as advanced online and electronic research capabilities

Benefits:

  • Annual health checkup
  • National Pension scheme
  • Meal Card (Sodexo)
  • Healthy Half (0.5 day leave every 6 months for wellbeing)
  • 22 days of annual leave  
  • Transportation allowance
  • Day care / Creche Reimbursement
  • Group Medical Claim
  • Access to LinkedIn Learning

Life at Janes

We believe Janes is truly a great place to work. Our values and leadership code drive everything we do, and we understand that the right behaviours and culture will always result in the best outcomes for our customers, our colleagues, our shareholders, and our business. We provide a supportive, stretching, and dynamic environment with the ability for you to grow rapidly, both personally and professionally.

Janes is an inclusive and equal opportunities employer and encourages applications regardless of age, race, disability, religion / belief, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, or gender.

Although this role is advertised as full time, Janes believed that flexibility at work can provide many significant benefits both to our colleagues and the business. We already work in a hybrid style across all offices and regions and can support different ways of working and offer different flexible working arrangements. So, if you are interested and have any requirements or needs in the way you would like to work, please apply, and speak to us about this.  We will always consider part time or flexible applications

jobs.title: Research Analyst - Market Forecast
jobs.description:
COMPANY

Janes enables militaries, governments, and defence companies to make critical decisions. Our expert-driven tradecraft, developed over 120 years, combined with human-machine teaming, delivers assured open-source intelligence across military capabilities and order of battle, equipment, events, countries, companies and markets.

Linking millions of assured data points, Janes data model creates a framework of interconnected open-source defence intelligence. This allows our customers to integrate all relevant data and connections into a single intelligence environment to deliver a more complete and accurate answer. Using Janes, our customers can use their scarce resource more effectively, to get to better decisions with higher confidence, more quickly. 

JOB PURPOSE

Janes is recruiting a Research Analyst for C4ISR & Weapons to be based in the Bangalore office in India. The Research Analyst will be involved in the domain of Aerospace & Defence and will be responsible for maintaining and updating our procurement and market forecast solutions.  

HOW YOU WILL CONTRIBUTE AT JANES

  • Produce material for Janes in an analytically rigorous fashion, taking into account industry insight, as well as related geopolitical, military, and economic factors.
  • Conduct market research (qualitative and quantitative), statistical analysis, and business intelligence to support our market forecast and defence procurement products.
  • Monitor news insights and press releases for further opportunities in defence procurement and the impact of policies in future market outlook.
  • Establish linkages and relationships between military C4ISR, Weapons and Platform programmes in terms of configuration, pricing, and operational requirements.
  • Identify and analyse contracts in the aerospace and defence domain and develop new market segments and functions.
  • Identify gaps in existing databases and conduct rigorous monthly audits with respect to various parameters across platform databases.
  • Prepare monthly reports documenting specific database changes and maintain worklogs.
  • Support a range of consulting, editorial and other analysis projects with true insights and demonstrate value to the Market Forecast portfolio.

THE IDEAL SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE FOR THIS ROLE ARE

  • A demonstrable interest in military platform markets, industry, technologies, and trends.
  • 1-3 years of experience in defence or defence-related sectors.
  • 1+ years of experience in the domain of market research and open source intelligence with a view of tracking contracts, tenders, and price information. 
  • Experience of working within a data environment (databases, data transformation, data validation or similar).
  • Computer competency that includes a thorough working knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint applications. Experience in Power BI would be an added advantage.
  • Proven record of undertaking project-orientated work to strict deadlines, lead projects, engage with industry and manage large volumes of content.
  • Ability to quickly adapt and work with new information technology applications.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills including editorial, consulting exposure and public speaking.
  • Excellent attention to detail and a logical approach to problem solving as well as ability to identify critical aspects of military platform markets, such as industrial capacity, technical characteristics and the competitive environment.
  • Qualifications - Bachelor’s degree in related disciplines such as economics, international relations & politics, natural sciences, engineering or data science. A Master’s degree would also be advantageous.

BENEFITS

India

  • Annual health checkup
  • National Pension scheme
  • Meal Card (Sodexo)
  • Healthy Half (0.5 day leave every 6 months for wellbeing) 
  • 22 days of annual leave  
  • Transportation allowance
  • Day care / Creche Reimbursement
  • Group Medical Claim
  • Access to LinkedIn Learning

Life at Janes

We believe Janes is truly a great place to work. Our values and leadership code drive everything we do, and we understand that the right behaviours and culture will always result in the best outcomes for our customers, our colleagues, our shareholders, and our business. We provide a supportive, stretching, and dynamic environment with the ability for you to grow rapidly, both personally and professionally.

Janes is an inclusive and equal opportunities employer and encourages applications regardless of age, race, disability, religion / belief, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, or gender.

  • Attention to detail and a logical approach to problem solving as well as ability to identify critical aspects of land platform markets, such as industrial capacity, technical characteristics and the competitive environment.

jobs.title: Research Analyst - Unmanned Systems
jobs.description:
Janes Introduction:

Janes empowers military, government, and defence leaders to act with confidence in an increasingly complex world. Our trusted defence, security, and geopolitical information delivered through seamless digital platforms and system integrations—turns overwhelming data into clear, actionable intelligence and insight. By filling critical information gaps, Janes helps customers analyse threats, accelerate decisions, and stay ahead of emerging challenges.

Job purpose:

Unmanned systems have become one of the fastest‑growing and most technically challenging domains in defence intelligence. With rapid global proliferation, especially across modern conflict zones, the rapid global uptake of unmanned systems is driving a sharp rise in the quantity, variety, and evolution rate of these new platforms.

Janes is seeking a highly motivated Research Analyst for defence unmanned platforms in  data‑driven environments. In this role, you will deliver robust research and analytical insights to support key projects across the unmanned systems domain.

How you will contribute at Janes:

You will be responsible for:

  • Gathering, creating, and integrating data from diverse sources into Janes’ unmanned systems database.
  • Collaborating with cross-functional teams (Air, Land, Sea, C4ISR and Weapons teams) to analyse and interpret equipment‑level data across unmanned platforms such as UAVs, USVs, and UGVs, and understand their interactions with other systems.
  • Staying abreast of emerging defence technologies relevant to unmanned systems, autonomy, sensors, and payloads.
  • Identifying gaps in the existing unmanned systems dataset and proposing solutions to improve coverage and accuracy.
  • Sourcing, validating, and verifying data points using primary and secondary research methods, aligned with Janes’ sourcing standards.
  • Conducting investigative research and engaging with Government agencies, industry partners, Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), and other relevant organisations to gather information when required.

The ideal skills and experience for this role are:

  • A strong team player with a demonstrable interest in the unmanned technology domain, backed by excellent research, analytical, and communication skills.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint; prior experience working in structured, data‑driven environments is highly desirable.
  • This role values candidates who can instinctively spot, classify, and interpret unmanned systems—whether through imagery, specifications, news flow, or technical documentation. Subject-specific training can be provided; however, experience in the defence industry or relevant military service will be considered an advantage.
  • Proven ability to build networks and conduct interviews via phone, email, or other communication channels.
  • Ability to interpret technical documents and secondary sources, analyse large volumes of data, and review imagery.
  • Mental flexibility and curiosity to conduct in‑depth investigative research when necessary.

Benefits

India

  • Annual health checkup
  • National Pension scheme
  • Meal Card (Sodexo)
  • Healthy Half (0.5 day leave every 6 months for wellbeing) 
  • 22 days of annual leave  
  • Transportation allowance
  • Day care / Creche Reimbursement
  • Group Medical Claim
  • Access to LinkedIn Learning

jobs.title: Associate, Capital Advisory
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International is seeking an Associate to join our growing Capital Advisory Team, based in Frankfurt. Successful candidates have excellent cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills, and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading M&A advisory firm. Successful Associates are motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, people oriented and entrepreneurial environment.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Technology, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2023, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 300 transactions worldwide.

The European Lincoln International Capital Advisory Group successfully advised on 350+ transactions worldwide since its inception in the range of Euro 50 to 350 million. In 2024 we had another record performance of our global Capital Advisory Group by providing creative and alternative financing solutions to clients out of 5 European offices with more than 25 dedicated professionals in order to secure capital for acquisition financings, investments, refinancings & recapitalisations, staple financings staving off defaults, restructuring businesses and navigating distressed situations and bankruptcies, and developing structured capital solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform.

  • Conduct market research and financial analysis to support client engagements
  • Support senior team members within the Capital Advisory Group (CAG) in Frankfurt in executing debt financing transactions (acquisition financing, refinancing and recapitalisation deals) as part of the wider European CAG team
  • Prepare marketing documents and pitches
  • Carry out financial analysis & modelling, including analysing business forecasts, scenario analysis and covenant sensitivities
  • Identify and contacting finance providers on transactions
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams both across Europe within the CAG team as well as local M&A teams to ensure seamless project execution
  • Attend client meetings and participate in discussions
  • Interact with senior bankers and clients’ senior executives
  • Leading or implementing initiatives focused on growing the firm’s franchise value

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Associate to develop as quickly as he/she is capable.

The following factors differentiate the offering at Lincoln International:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, working in small deal teams with extensive client exposure as well as cross border contexts
  • Introduction to a vast array of industries
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure, including to very seasoned and experienced senior bankers and provides an opportunity to make a valued contribution and have an impact
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels
  • Opportunity to participate in our US and European Secondment Programmes
  • Mentoring Scheme, onboarding sessions and structured developmental feedback
  • The chance to build your career within a growth environment.  

Qualifications:

The Associate position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • 3-4 years prior professional experience in Capital Advisory, Leveraged Finance, Transaction Services or Business Modelling experience, preferably in the mid market
  • Fluency and thorough understanding in Accounting, Corporate Finance and/or Leveraged Finance coupled with excellent Business Acumen
  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally educated to Masters level in a business related subject
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn
  • Solid and growing understanding of business models and sectors coupled with a good grasp of corporate strategy and themes related to company growth and development
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, underpinned through professional and academic excellence as well as other pursuits and personal projects
  • Adaptability, dependability and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear and engaging communication skills- verbally as well as written. Is able to relate and connect to other stakeholders quickly- clients, management teams and LI colleagues
  • Collaborative style and a personally engaging manner
  • International experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, other European Languages are a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.
