jobs.title: PM Associate, Private Credit
jobs.description:
PM Associate, Private Credit

Established in 1984, Bain Capital is one of the world's leading private investment firms with approximately $105 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital manages a range of strategies including private equity, public equity, credit, venture capital, real estate, life sciences, and impact investments.  Our competitive advantage is grounded in a diligence-intensive, value-added investment approach that enables the firm to deliver industry-leading returns for its investors. Headquartered in Boston, Bain Capital has offices in Chicago, Dublin, Guangzhou, Hong Kong, London, Madrid, Melbourne, Mumbai, Munich, New York, Palo Alto, San Francisco, Seoul, Shanghai, Sydney, and Tokyo.

BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $53 billion in assets under management.  We invest across the full spectrum of credit strategies, including asset-based finance, direct lending, leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, non-performing loans, real estate and other hard assets and equities.  Through a wide diversity of mandates, we have the ability to invest in every level of a company or asset’s capital structure from secured debt to equity and can provide holistic solutions to growing companies with unique financing needs.

Our investment process is characterized by detailed business and financial analysis and a culture of vigorous debate.  We combine a consulting-based approach to industry, competitor, and business analysis with an in-depth examination of a target company’s financial performance and capital structure.  Our goal is to select the best industries, the best companies, and the best relative value among the many securities each company offers.

Bain Capital Credit has a world-class team of over 110 investment professionals with extensive experience analyzing and managing high-yield investments. 

PRIVATE CREDIT GROUP

Bain Capital Credit’s Private Credit Group evaluates investment opportunities across the spectrum in the middle market, or typically companies with $10-150M of EBITDA located in North America, Europe and Asia Pacific.  With a dedicated global team of over 40 investment professionals, we can lead investments across the capital structure in senior secured first lien, second lien and unitranche loans, subordinated debt, and preferred or common equity co-investments. Our dedicated global team affords us the ability to diligence the most complex situations and provides private capital to those companies. 

PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT TEAM

The Portfolio Management Team works directly with senior management on group strategy and fund positioning, with a focus on asset allocation, return optimization, risk management, liability management and fund analysis. The Private Credit Group has $21 billion AUM today across two business development companies, Bain Capital Specialty Finance, Inc. (NYSE: BCSF) and Bain Capital Private Credit (BCPC), the flagship Global Direct Lending and Middle Market Credit Partners funds, as well as several other separately managed accounts, Private Credit CLOs and structured fund products. We are looking to hire an Associate.

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE

  • Analyze current portfolio positioning and capitalization across funds and managed accounts in the firm’s Private Credit strategies

  • Support in evaluating, structuring, and executing on funds, structured funds (rated notes, collateralized fund obligations), financing structures (SPVs, CLOs) and other structured products

  • Contribute to the assessment and execution of strategic growth planning initiatives within the Private Credit Group

  • Build and maintain complex financial models of the Private Credit Group’s funds and managed accounts

  • Generate the analytical framework used by the portfolio management team, investors, and other related parties

  • Conduct a wide range of research and analysis for use in portfolio management, investor requests and internal analytics

  • Communicate with senior management and investors on current portfolio construction and return information

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials and a demonstrated track record of professional high performance

  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience with a strong preference for prior credit market experience including leveraged lending, private credit or structured credit experience

  • Excellent communication skills; able to synthesize complicated analyses concisely and articulate conclusions in a compelling manner.  Open to spirited debate over ideas.

  • Strong financial statement analysis and modeling abilities. Strong understanding of public and private capital markets, corporate securities and capital structures

  • High agility and an ability to multi-task and work across the organization on a wide range of workstreams and deliverables.

  • Experience with middle market direct lending, or CLO or other structured / financing products is a plus

Compensation:
Expected Annual Base Salary $140,000-150,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Aviation Asset Management Associate
jobs.description:
With approximately $205 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit & Capital Markets, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,950+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.

BAIN CAPITAL SPECIAL SITUATIONS OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Special Situations has $24 billion in assets under management and has invested $19 billion in ~250 deals across a balanced geographic mix of investments in North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific since its founding in 2018. We provide bespoke capital solutions to meet the diverse needs of companies, entrepreneurs, and asset owners - in all market cycles. The strategy brings together equity and credit expertise, as well as corporate and real asset expertise, to provide solutions which cannot be met by traditional providers or others funds within Bain Capital. Our dedicated, global team of approximately 145 investment and portfolio professionals contribute the local expertise and capabilities that enable these diverse investments.

Aviation is one of our key focus areas, both today and through the market cycle. Over our history, we have invested close to $5 billion in Special Situations aviation investments. Acquiring physical aircraft, including commercial, cargo, and private jets, has been a particular focus with over $2 billion invested in this sub-sector over the past few years. Going forward, we expect to build off our industry-leading track record to continue to grow our presence in aircraft hard assets, including investing dedicated capital for this investment strategy.  

PORTFOLIO GROUP OVERVIEW

Bain Capital has a dedicated global Portfolio Group within the firm’s Special Situations business. The Portfolio Group is responsible for fund and asset management and leads the strategy and analysis on fund construction, risk, and opportunities for the Global Special Situations business.

The Portfolio Group works side-by-side with Special Situations leadership and deal teams and provides extensive support, whenever and wherever needed on analytical, strategic, and operating issues. These capabilities have helped many Bain Capital companies develop and execute strategies to move swiftly to gain competitive advantage and achieve superior growth. We currently own ~$7 billion of aircraft assets across several platforms. Given this scale and complexity, the Portfolio Group is a highly valued and fully integrated piece of our aircraft strategy. We are currently hiring an Associate to work as part of the Portfolio Group focused on aircraft hard assets in our New York City office.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide analytical support to inform investment decisions, including new asset acquisitions, financings, sales, etc.
  • Act as a thought partner to the deal team, leveraging modeling, data, and analytics
  • Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders around all key asset management actions
  • Work on all aspects of the portfolio, including new deals, ongoing portfolio work, and ultimately value realization
  • Focus on modeling, data analytics, industry research, and internal/external reporting, all with the goal of optimizing investment outcomes
  • Develop analytical tools and maintain systems to assist in strategy setting, returns optimization, and capital formation
  • Attend board meetings, global industry events, etc.

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials (BS/BS in finance, economics, accounting and/or related degree preferred)
  • 2-4 years of data analytics, risk analytics, portfolio management, or strategic finance experience; prior background in aviation or aircraft leasing is a strong plus but not required
  • High proficiency in Excel; ability to build and iterate analytical tools and dashboard with experience through former roles, internships or coursework in managing datasets and running financial models / scenario analytics
  • Ability to synthesize large, ambiguous data sets into clear insights
  • High proficiency in Powerpoint with ability to effectively communicate insights from analyses to diverse stakeholders
  • Strong interpersonal skills with ability to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm
  • Strong oral and written communication skills with ability to distill analysis into crisp recommendations

Compensation

Expected Base Salary Range: $125,000 – $150,000

This is the expected base salary range for this position. Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Business Systems Director, Private Equity
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. 

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

For more information visit: Bain Capital

DESCRIPTION

This position will be working within Bain Capital’s Private Markets and Corporate Technology team, which supports  Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Real Assets and cross firm platform services areas. This role will work on systems and projects focused on supporting needs of global private equity business.

Leveraging both your business and technical knowledge, this individual will work closely with business stakeholders, vendors, application developers and QA specialists to provide solutions supporting our technology platforms for private equity deal , portfolio group ,  finance and investment operations.

This will include driving PE mandated projects as well as participating at various levels in other technology related initiatives, from strategic approach conception to new system development/integration to post implementation work. The ideal candidate will possess strong leadership skills, a sense of ownership, exceptional communication skills, and a deep understanding of private markets focused systems and processes.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone to use their knowledge to really make an impact across a wide spectrum of businesses and functions, and to gain exposure to key business leaders in a world class organization.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Business Analysis & Delivery

  • Lead requirements gathering, documentation, and testing of solutions supporting global private equity business needs.
  • Translate complex business requirements into actionable technical specifications.
  • Identify opportunities to streamline processes and enhance system functionality.
  • Oversee applications supporting the PE business  including  deal management, fund management,  accounting, portfolio reporting 

Stakeholder Engagement & Communication

  • Partner with deal, portfolio , Finance and operations teams to align technology solutions with business goals.
  • Compile and communicate release notes, roadmaps, and project updates for stakeholders.
  • Serve as a trusted advisor, balancing business priorities with IT objectives.

Vendor & Product Ownership

  • Help Manage application support vendors and third-party system providers.
  • Ensure vendor solutions align with Bain Capital’s technology standards and business needs.
  • Oversee software release cycles and coordinate delivery across internal and external teams.

Continuous Improvement & Subject Matter Expertise

  • Act as a subject matter expert on private markets deal, operations, finance and other associated  processes.
  • Support development of new integrations and upgrades to existing platforms.
  • Advocate best practices in systems analysis, product management, and project governance.

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Finance, or related field.
  • 10+ years of business/systems analysis experience supporting  buy-side/ asset management firms
  • Demonstrated prior experience supporting private equity / Growth type businesses
  • Deep understanding of the end-to-end private equity lifecycle across multiple funds, strategies, and geographies
  • Proven track record of managing multiple projects and driving business-facing technology solutions.
  • Excellent communication skills with ability to engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments.
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to understand complex business requirements and translate them into technical solutions.
  • Familiarity with project reporting tools and release management practices.
  • Technical skills with SQL, Excel, Python, or BI/reporting tools.
  • Experience with Investran or an equivalent private equity accounting solution is preferred
  • Experience with Chronograph or an equivalent portfolio data collection solution is preferred
  • Experience with evaluating, implementing /configuring  AI enabled solutions to augment key business processes is preferred 

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $190,000 - $215,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. PRIVATE EQUITY OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Partners is the private equity affiliate of Bain Capital. Over the past 31 years, The Private Equity Group has made over 280 investments and has 15 active global buyout funds. Our private equity activity primarily includes leveraged buyouts and growth capital invested across a wide variety of sectors. Bain Capital has found that a combination of a strong management team, sound fundamental business analysis, and a focused strategy can substantially improve a company’s income as well as its long-term strategic value.

Bain Capital Partners seeks opportunities to invest in market leading companies poised for dynamic change and to back outstanding management teams to implement that change. We believe the most critical ingredients in a company’s success are the management team and the alignment of management incentives with those of the investors. Our portfolio companies have significant management-equity ownership as a key part of the investment strategy. Bain Capital professionals are significant investors in each transaction, which achieves an alignment of goals with portfolio company management teams.

The group has developed significant expertise in several business areas including technology, healthcare, retail, telecommunications, and industrial and consumer products.  Transaction types include leveraged acquisitions, expansion capital, growth buyouts, turnarounds, consolidation plays, private buyouts, public company recapitalizations, convertible preferred investments, and financial restructurings.

jobs.title: 2027 Summer Analyst, Real Estate
jobs.description:
Bain Capital Overview

With approximately $205 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit & Capital Markets, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,960+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.


We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.
 

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes.

Real Estate Overview

Since 2010, senior members of the Bain Capital Real Estate (“BCRE”) team have refined a thematic and nimble business model, focused on thesis-driven and resilient portfolio construction, designed to capture long-term demand growth and changing customer needs and preferences for real estate. Our team, which is led by Ryan Cotton, Partner, and comprises over 100 dedicated professionals, has been thoughtfully constructed to capture market opportunities at scale. To date, our strategy has been utilized for the acquisition of ~700 assets with total equity commitments of $10.7 billion. This includes, but is not limited to, acquisitions across BCRE Fund I ($1.5 billion, 2018 vintage), BCRE Fund II ($3.0 billion, 2020 vintage), the BCRE Life Science Fund (~$800 million, 2021 vintage), and BCRE Fund III (Raise completed in 2025, 2023 vintage) value add-plus real estate funds focused predominantly on thematic investments in North America. We believe that while our business model is built to succeed in a broad range of environments, it is particularly well suited for the current period of volatility, with distress likely to create attractive deal flow over the near to medium term.

Averaging over 20 years of applicable experience, Bain Capital Real Estate’s 12 Partners and Managing Directors have long and varied careers creating and managing innovative real estate and private equity investment platforms across an array of investment environments. Our senior team approaches decision-making with their expertise in acquisitions, asset management, portfolio management, financing, investment sales, investment banking, financial reporting, legal, engineering, and development management, in mind. They also have direct lines into long-time industry leaders as well as up-and-coming real estate players across a variety of asset classes and specialties. The team draws upon this foundation of complementary expertise and experience to source opportunities, drive value, and mitigate risk. As part of Bain Capital, the real estate team leverages the collective scale, experience, and network of the firm’s global platform to augment sourcing efforts as well as to deepen insights and understanding of underlying secular themes driving demand and changing customer needs and desires across sectors germane to its current portfolio as well as future investment themes.

Position Description

The Summer Analyst role is a 10-week Boston-based opportunity. Bain Capital professionals are exposed to a broad set of situations and companies, and systematically develop skills as a value-added investor - not just a financial engineer. Summer Analysts will learn how to evaluate investment opportunities through the application of fundamental strategic analysis in addition to the more traditional financial modeling.

Ideal candidates will have a strong academic record. Candidates should exhibit the following skills and/or experiences:

  • Self-motivated, creative thinker with strong commercial instincts
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Demonstrated ability to think creatively
  • Excellent academic record from a top undergraduate school

Compensation

Expected Weekly Base Salary: $2,100

This is the expected weekly base salary range for this position. Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Vice President - Originations
jobs.description:
We at Bridge acknowledge and appreciate the uniqueness of each individual, understanding that our people are the key to our success. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels respected and valued. Our dedication extends beyond our business goals to making a positive impact in our communities and upholding Sustainability and Responsibility (S&R) principles at our properties and corporate locations. We evaluate all candidates based on merit, without any discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by law. As an equal opportunity employer, we are devoted to creating an inclusive hiring process. We actively work to eliminate barriers and provide reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities. Our goal is to provide fair opportunities and access throughout the recruitment, hiring, and employment experience. Your specific needs are important to us. If you need accommodations related to a disability or any other protected status during the recruitment process, please inform us.

Make an impact

Bridge Debt Strategies is seeking a motivated VP of Originations to support the sourcing, underwriting, and execution of direct lending investments. This individual will work closely with senior leaders to evaluate opportunities, manage transactions from initial review through closing, and support portfolio performance throughout the life of each investment.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Focus on sourcing, underwriting, structuring, and closing real estate debt investments, working closely with senior originators and Investment Management Committee members
  • Develop and maintain strong relationships with sponsors/borrowers, mortgage brokers, and other intermediaries in order to source new direct lending opportunities
  • Travel to borrower meetings, property tours, and industry events to support deal sourcing and execution
  • Underwrite potential direct lending opportunities by modeling and reviewing detailed financial models, including cash flow projections, value creation, lease-up, and refinance or sale exit scenarios
  • Analyze credit risk, sponsorship strength, market fundamentals, and collateral quality for each investment
  • Coordinate the originations, transaction management and deal teams through underwriting, investment management committee approval, and closing
  • Lead the preparation of loan investment summaries and internal credit memos for presentation to the Investment Management Committee
  • Act as a resource to the asset management team post-closing, providing underwriting insight and support on amendments, extensions, and ongoing credit matters

What you should bring

  • 3 to 8 years of experience in real estate lending/originations, debt underwriting, investment banking and/or capital markets. Title is TBD based on experience.
  • Bachelor’s degree from a top-tier university
  • Strong credit, structuring and underwriting experience across all commercial real estate property types, with a focus on transitional assets
  • Ability to independently lead underwriting, structuring, and execution of real estate debt investments
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong focus on deal execution and relationship building
  • Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills
  • Ability to manage multiple transactions simultaneously and meet aggressive timelines
  • Proven ability to work independently while collaborating effectively in a team-oriented environment

What you can be a part of

Eligible employees are automatically enrolled at a 4% contribution rate. *The employee must be at least 21 years of age and have worked for the Company for at least 60 days.

jobs.title: PhD Degree Required - Quantitative Analyst/Programmer, Mortgage Trading Desk (RMBS)
jobs.description:
Founded in 1992, Cerberus is a global leader in alternative investing with approximately $65 billion in assets across complementary credit, private equity, and real estate strategies. We invest across the capital structure where our integrated investment platforms and proprietary operating capabilities create an edge to improve performance and drive long-term value. Our tenured teams have experience working collaboratively across asset classes, sectors, and geographies to seek strong risk-adjusted returns for our investors. For more information about our people and platforms, visit us at www.cerberus.com.

Job Purpose:

Cerberus Capital Management (CCM) is recruiting graduates of PhD programs to join our mortgage trading desk as a Quantitative Analyst/Programmer. You will work directly with trading desk to engineer and optimize data processing, model development, trading processes and interface developments.  You will sit on the trading desk and provide insights to portfolio managers and the broader investment team.

We seek a professional with a PhD degree in a quantitative field such as math/statistics, physics, mechanical/electrical engineering, or operations research.

Responsibilities & Deliverables:

  • Explore and develop analytical tools to analyze large datasets of various asset classes including RMBS and asset-backed securities
  • Work with traders/portfolio managers directly to implement and test various models
  • Partner with other developers/programmers on the team on larger scale projects
  • Participate in real time transaction-oriented projects
  • Participate in various portfolio management projects, such as position and risk management systems

Key Requirements:

  • PhD degree in science and engineering discipline, such as math/statistics, physics, mechanical/electrical engineering, or operations research
  • Strong analytical and math skills
  • Fluent in at least one programming language
  • Experiences working with large data sets: SQL or python a plus

The base salary for this position is expected to be between $140,000.00 and $215,000.00. The base salary offered to the chosen candidate will be commensurate with a candidate’s relevant experience and other qualifications for the position, as determined by the Company in its sole discretion. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for an annual discretionary bonus, which is often a meaningful portion of the compensation package, and a robust benefits package.

  • Prepare detailed research reports highlighting key findings, investment theses, financial forecasts, catalysts, and valuation analyses
  • Conduct thorough due diligence by engaging with company management and industry experts
  • Monitor industry news and stay updated on company developments
  • Assist in conducting comprehensive industry and company research
  • Respond promptly to sales and client requests for information and analysis
  • Participate in company briefings, industry events, roadshows, and conferences

Requirements

  • Degree holder (graduated) in mathematics, statistic, finance or related discipline
  • Minimum 2 years of full-time relevant experience in equity research
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with the ability to analyse complex financial data and make informed decisions
  • Proficient in financial modelling and different valuation techniques, while embracing innovation and new technologies to enhance the research process
  • Self-motivated, diligent‎, multi-tasking with high standards for excellence and attention to details
  • Highly proficient in excel and good numeracy skills
  • Well rounded, and a team player
  • Self-motivated and able to work under pressure
  • Fluency in both written and verbal English, with Chinese language is a plus but not essential
  • Immediate available is highly preferred
  • Experience in healthcare or biopharmaceutical sector is preferred but not a must

jobs.title: Associate, Product Marketing, Global Private Wealth
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking. 

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences. 

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm. 

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer. 

This position is based in our EMEA Headquarters in London and will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed. 

The Associate is a member of the Global Private Wealth marketing organization, responsible for supporting the organization’s Product Marketing activities, with a focus on both new product launches and management of existing products. 

This role partners closely with Investor Relations and client-facing teams to ensure product materials, presentations, and diligence documentation are accurate, coordinated, and aligned to fundraising priorities. The position plays a central role in supporting the launch of new products from a product marketing and sales enablement perspective and maintaining high standards across ongoing product management. 

The ideal candidate is someone who has: 

  • Experience in the wealth marketplace and private markets. 

  • Familiarity across fundraising workflows and investor diligence processes. 

  • Experience with evergreen structures and/or fund of funds strongly preferred. 

What you will do: 

Product Launch & Commercial Readiness 

  • Support product marketing and sales enablement execution for new product launches within the private wealth platform. 

  • Contribute to the development of core product marketing collateral, including pitchbooks, standard DDQs, datarooms, talking points, and other platform-specific materials. 

  • Partner across Product, Investment, Compliance, and Investor Relations to ensure timely, accurate, and consistent launch execution. 

  • Maintain structured launch tracking and update Functional and Regional Team Leads on product launch progress. 

Sales & Product Enablement 

  • Adapt materials to meet private bank, wealth platform, and advisory firm requirements. 

  • Respond to evolving fundraising priorities through timely updates and customized presentation support. 

  • Contribute to the maintenance of core product marketing collateral. 

  • Contribute to the maintenance of slide and presentation libraries and product marketing documentation repositories. 

RFP, DDQ & Due Diligence 

  • Lead responses to RFPs, DDQs, and investor diligence requests. 

  • Contribute to the maintenance of structured response libraries. 

  • Gather and validate technical inputs from investment and product teams. 

  • Support management of data rooms and investor review processes. 

Ongoing Product Governance 

  • Maintain accuracy and consistency across product materials, presentations, and diligence documentation. 

  • Ensure alignment between approved product narratives and client-facing materials across regions. 

  • Implement updates following product developments, performance changes, or structural enhancements. 

  • Incorporate feedback from Investor Relations and client-facing teams to strengthen materials over time. 

  • And other responsibilities as required 

What you bring: 

  • Experience working within the wealth marketplace required. 

  • Familiarity with private markets products and fundraising workflows. 

  • Experience with evergreen structures is strongly preferred. 

  • Experience in responding to RFPs, DDQs, and client inquiries. 

  • Strong presentation development, analytical, and project coordination skills. 

  • Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment. 

  • Experience with sales enablement or content management platforms (e.g., Seismic, Loopio, or similar) a plus. 

Education preferred: 

  • Bachelor of Science (B.S) / Bachelor of Arts (B.A) or equivalent experience 

Experience: 

  • 4-6 years of experience in private markets or financial product marketing, sales enablement, product specialist, capital raising support, or related roles.

jobs.title: Vice President, Evergreen Private Equity Investment Specialist (Boston)
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

Except for work travel, this position is expected to be in the Boston office 4 days minimum per week. There will also be a need for travel.

About the role:

Vice President, Investment Specialist will work to drive the growth of HarbourVest’s private equity evergreen solutions in the United States.

HarbourVest’s evergreen solutions include private markets comingled vehicles and separate account opportunities, targeted at private wealth and institutional investors. Responsibilities include working alongside Investor Relations and Private Wealth teams to facilitate new business wins, identifying capability builds that cross-functionally improve the product and identifying adjacencies for new product development. This individual will be responsible for educating and engaging with the global Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams and other internal partners; making pitches to clients and prospects; conducting portfolio reviews with clients and overseeing important client responses to ensure quality of submissions.

The ideal candidate is someone who has:

  • A strong understanding of private market evergreen vehicles and a deep knowledge of private equity and the private wealth market.
  • Excellent presentation and client engagement skills with the ability to tailor information based on the audience.
  • Strong relationship management skills to partner closely with fellow HarbourVest Investment and Investor Relations professionals, as well as external prospects and clients.
  • Strong investment and analytical competence.
  • Strong written skills.
  • A high level of maturity and ability to multi-task with little direct supervision.
  • An entrepreneurial approach with an ability to be forward-thinking and work without a playbook.
  • A desire for travel.
  • An understanding of the investment process.
  • A strong understanding of quantitative investment measurements and statistical tools.

What you will do:

External responsibilities:

  • Support sales, by bringing in-depth vehicle and market expertise to prospect and client meetings.
  • Support the Investor Relations teams in their discussions with private wealth prospects, clients, distribution partners and institutional investors. Demonstrate deep knowledge of the market, product, HarbourVest’s capabilities, solution and differentiation.
  • Take into account the broader picture; engage externally around a broad range of business and investment topics. Speak with nuance around the investment landscape, client portfolio and market outlook.
  • Display creativity and problem solving in proposing solutions to client challenges. Consider the wider business implications and context (complexity, operational impact) in discussions that will result in the optimal outcome for HarbourVest.
  • Deep understanding of key competitors, fundraising and industry trends.

Internal product specialist responsibilities:

  • Engage with the Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams to collect feedback from the market. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence and apply it to product evolution and content development.
  • Project manage the sales opportunity set and go-to-market strategy for US private equity evergreen products. Act as liaison point for all evergreen collective sales efforts, organized across various teams for the product.
  • Partner with the Investor Relations team to serve as the main point of contact to arrange calls/meetings, follow-ups, comprehensive investment knowledge, identity and address concerns and provide guidance through diligence.
  • Educate the Private Client and Investor Relations teams on the product and strategy.
  • Oversee important RFPs, written collateral, portfolio updates and client responses to ensure the quality of submissions.

Education Preferred:

Strong academic and professional credentials. MBA and / or CFA preferred but not required.

Experience:

10+ years of investment or product roles with a client service orientation. An investment research background is a plus. Must have a blend of product leadership, investment capabilities and commercial sense. Relevant backgrounds include product specialist for liquid products, investment banking, equity analyst, consulting or transaction services experience. Spanish language fluency is an asset.

Evergreen Solutions Team Description

Guided by a clear and ambitious mission, the Evergreen Solutions Team is enabling HarbourVest to become the leading multimanager provider of private markets focused evergreen solutions for wealth and institutional investors globally. Our team blends deep investment expertise with industry‑leading data, technology, and portfolio management capabilities to deliver seamless, scalable solutions for institutions and wealth partners. We collaborate across HarbourVest to build purpose‑built tools, streamline operations, and deliver transparent insights that support thoughtful allocation and long‑term portfolio construction.

#LI-Hybrid

Salary Range

$117,000.00 - $163,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This role follows a hybrid work model which consists of 4 days in office per week. Additionally, you will receive 5 additional remote days per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval.  

HarbourVest Partners is a leading global private markets investment firm with over four decades of experience delivering innovative solutions to institutional and high-net-worth clients. As we expand our presence across the institutional segment in APAC, we are seeking an Investor Relations Client Marketing Director to elevate our brand visibility and promote our strategies in key markets across the region.

In this newly-created role, the Marketing Director will be responsible for developing and implementing integrated marketing strategies tailored to the institutional channel, ensuring consistent messaging that resonates with local audiences and supports our regional fundraising goals.

The ideal candidate is someone who is:

  • Adept at marketing communication tools and channels

  • Proven in their ability to lead impactful marketing campaigns

  • Exceptionally organized with strong attention to detail

  • Collaborative and able to work effectively with cross-functional teams

  • Driven to achieve results

What you will do:

  • Develop and implement integrated marketing plans tailored to institutional channels across APAC, aligning all activities with fundraising objectives and regional priorities.

  • Lead multi-tactic campaigns across digital, content, events, web, and sponsorships, ensuring brand consistency and compliance with local regulatory requirements.

  • Incorporate content creation, third-party sponsorships, email, and web into campaign planning to enhance engagement and impact across all marketing touchpoints.

  • Collaborate and develop strong relationships with key stakeholders across the APAC Investor Relations team, investment teams, global marketing, and external partners.

  • Track, measure, and report on the efficiency of marketing activities

  • And other responsibilities as required

What you bring:

  • Experience in asset management, financial services, or private equity marketing

  • Proven experience working in global organizations and collaborating across geographies

  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Education Preferred:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

Experience:

  • 8-10 years of experience in B2B marketing, with specific expertise in implementing marketing campaigns across paid, earned, and owned media

jobs.title: Vice President, Client Marketing – Global Private Wealth
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking. In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences. We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position is based in our new Boston Headquarters and will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

The Vice President, Client Marketing leads the Client Marketing pillar within HarbourVest’s Global Private Wealth marketing organization. This newly created role sits at the center of how the firm supports fundraising across global wealth audiences.

The VP owns campaigns, events / sponsorships, lifecycle communications, and the collateral supporting Investor Relations and distribution teams across the prospect and client journey. With a global directive spanning North America, EMEA, and APAC, the role translates commercial priorities and segmentation strategy into targeted marketing efforts, ensuring consistent execution in partnership with regional and corporate marketing teams.

This is a leadership opportunity to develop and scale how HarbourVest brings private markets solutions to wealth audiences globally.

Core Responsibilities

IR Partnership & Commercial Alignment

  • Lead the marketing partnership with GPW Investor Relations and regional distribution leadership.
  • Align campaign priorities and execution plans to fundraising objectives and pipeline needs.
  • Translate segmentation strategy and commercial priorities into actionable marketing plans, audience targeting, and campaign sequencing.

Campaign Leadership

  • Own the global campaign calendar targeting wealth audiences.
  • Develop and implement integrated campaigns with a digital center of gravity (email, web, paid, virtual programs).
  • Ensure campaigns are properly targeted, sequenced, and measured in alignment with defined audience segments and fundraising priorities.

Events & Field Activation

  • Lead strategy and coordination of webinars, virtual programs, partner initiatives, sponsorships, and selective in-person events aligned to distribution priorities.
  • Integrate events and sponsorships into broader campaign strategies, including pre-event targeting and post-event follow-up.
  • Ensure presentation materials, event collateral, and communications directly support pipeline development and client engagement.

Lifecycle Communications & Collateral

  • Define required communications and materials across the prospect-to-client lifecycle.
  • Ensure client-facing teams are equipped with accurate, current collateral to support prospecting, onboarding, and ongoing engagement.
  • Incorporate field feedback into ongoing refinement of campaigns and materials.

Operating Model & Governance

  • Establish and manage the client marketing operating model, including intake, prioritization, execution oversight, and reporting.
  • Oversee campaign and collateral workflows through required review and compliance processes.
  • Deliver structured performance reporting and insights to marketing and distribution leadership.

Team Leadership & Global Collaboration

  • Over time, build and lead a Client Marketing team for Global Private Wealth.
  • Partner closely with international and corporate marketing teams to deliver consistent execution across regions while respecting local requirements.
  • Set standards for campaign execution, event / sponsorship coordination, and lifecycle collateral globally.

Qualifications

  • 12–15+ years of experience in global wealth or intermediary distribution marketing within asset management, alternatives, wealth platforms, or adjacent sectors.
  • Demonstrated experience translating commercial strategy and segmentation into targeted campaigns and measurable marketing outcomes.
  • Proven track record leading wealth-focused campaigns, events / sponsorships, and sales-support collateral.
  • Strong digital marketing fluency and experience delivering multi-channel campaigns.
  • Experience partnering closely with IR, commercial, or distribution leadership.
  • Proven leadership experience and ability to operate effectively across regions and time zones.
  • Private markets and alternatives experience preferred; familiarity with evergreen structures a plus.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills and executive presence.

#LI-Hybrid

Salary Range

$160,000.00 - $240,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income will be the primary developer and maintainer of internal systems critical to the daily management of our successful and growing Fixed Income ETF lineup. The Analyst will work under the direction of ETF Portfolio Management to operate and extend tools for automated trading, performance attribution, corporate action analysis, basket negotiation tools and data quality monitoring, among other duties. Successful candidates will be self-directed, curious and eager to identify and implement opportunities to improve the portfolio management process. Knowledge of Python and SQL is required. The ability to work in a group environment with daily interactions with portfolio managers, information technology, and operational staff is expected. General knowledge of ETFs, currency hedging, fixed income securities, trading strategies, broker relationships and portfolio management functions is a plus, but not required. The position, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, will serve as a single contributor with no immediate direct reports. 

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Develop, support and maintain our proprietary risk, flow and order optimization/monitoring system.
  • Own our In-Kind Order negotiation and capture process, working directly with Brokers, Portfolio Management, Trading and Technology to enhance our capabilities.
  • Develop a full understanding of our investment process; become capable of handling orders and rebalances with minimal supervision.
  • Gain a thorough understanding of the global ETF infrastructure and workflow, which includes Portfolio Management requirements for ETF portfolios managed in Europe, Asia, and the United States.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, physics, mathematics, or electrical engineering, or other scientific discipline preferred.
  • Prior experience with Python and SQL is required. Experience with JavaScript (esp. React/Angular), Rust, C, C++, Java, C# or similar is also valuable.
  • Experience with Polars, Plotly Dash (or equivalent), Flask, and the standard python data science stack.
  • Basic knowledge of git is required. Experience with CI tools (GitHub/BitBucket pipelines, Jenkins) is a plus.
  • Working knowledge of Linear Programming at the undergraduate level is desirable. Experience with libraries such as Gurobi/CPLEX/SCIP is a huge plus.
  • Linux experience, or experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.) is a plus.
  • Fixed Income/Trading experience is desirable but not required.
  • Ability to make quick decisions, establish priorities and concentrate on detailed information under time sensitive conditions.
  • Knowledge of the global fixed income markets, prior trading experience, and the ability to provide market color are preferred skills but not required.
  • Proficiency in any of the following PM systems is considered a plus: Aladdin, Bloomberg, Charles River.

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $125,000 to $147,000 annually. The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable U.S. state and federal laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. What we are we looking for:  

Invesco is seeking a Senior Associate, Product Management, to support our firm’s Private Credit team! Our European private credit capabilities are rapidly expanding and we are seeking a motivated, curious, independent, and hard-working individual to join a dynamic team in our London office.

You will primarily focus on supporting business development strategy initiatives for our European private credit business across broadly syndicated loans, CLO, direct lending and special situations. You will work closely with our private credit team, including Portfolio Managers, Client Portfolio Managers and fellow Product Managers, as well as other functions across the firm including distribution, marketing, compliance, operations and other key business support functions.

Responsibilities include:

  • Helping with launches of new capabilities, product positioning, product research & competitive trends, and support of overall product management initiatives.

  • Managing and supporting marketing collateral related to private credit capabilities

  • Assisting in crafting responses to RFP/RFI questionnaires

  • Collaborating with team members on strategic and ad-hoc projects

  • Originating and coordinating responses to client inquires related to capital raising and ongoing relationship management

  • Preparing client presentations, reporting and marketing materials

  • Working with team members to liaise with sales force to ensure timely and high-quality client deliverables

  • Participating in thought leadership initiatives including commentaries, whitepapers, and other media

You should have an understanding of private credit markets and concepts. Strong communication, intellectual curiosity, problem solving and organizational skills are core requirements for the position.

You will need a strong knowledge of the investment philosophy and process of each investment strategy supported. This includes a detailed understanding of current investment themes within the portfolio, knowledge of the portfolio holdings, risk attributes of the strategy, past and current positioning, near-term outlook for the strategy and performance.

jobs.title: Associate, Private Credit
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. Your job:

We’re looking for an Associate – Private Credit to join our Bank Loans team in London!

This is an exciting opportunity to work across senior secured loans and high‑yield bonds while developing deep sector expertise, driving fundamental credit analysis, and contributing directly to portfolio construction. If you enjoy analytical work, want to deepen your understanding of leveraged finance, and thrive in a collaborative investment environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Among your responsibilities will be:

Investment Analysis & Credit Underwriting

  • Review and prepare credit theses for new senior secured loan and high‑yield bond investments (fixed and floating).

  • Produce detailed credit proposals, including company and industry analysis, capital structure assessment, cash‑flow projections, collateral adequacy, management quality, lender and sponsor strength, and restructuring considerations.

  • Present well‑argued investment recommendations to the Investment Committee.

  • Develop clear views on entry and exit yields for both existing and potential investments.

Sector Coverage & Monitoring

  • Build in‑depth knowledge across 2–3 industries and maintain full coverage of all senior loan and high‑yield opportunities within those sectors.

  • Work toward taking responsibility for 25–30 loan and bond positions.

  • Conduct ongoing monitoring, including financial analysis, cash‑flow modelling, rating reviews, collateral assessments, and performance tracking.

  • Identify relative value opportunities and make informed buy/sell/hold recommendations.

  • Actively generate new investment ideas within your coverage universe.

Market Interaction & Due Diligence

  • Participate in management meetings, site visits, plant tours, and inspections.

  • Engage with syndicate desks, sell‑side analysts, and private equity sponsors to evaluate both primary and secondary investment opportunities.

  • Review loan and bond documentation for new transactions and amendments, in collaboration with senior analysts and team leaders.

  • Build and maintain a strong network of peers to understand market sentiment on amendments and new deals.

Distressed & Special Situations

  • Assist in the workout and restructuring of distressed positions.

  • Support senior team members in evaluating complex secondary and stressed opportunities.

Risk Management & Administration

  • Maintain accurate electronic credit files, compliance reports, collateral records, and exposure reporting.

  • Ensure all activities align with Invesco’s Conduct Principles.

What you can bring:

  • Experience as a credit, leveraged finance, or corporate finance analyst (buy-side, sell-side, rating agency, PE, consulting, or similar).

  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Word.

  • Bachelor’s degree required; CFA qualification beneficial.

  • Additional European language(s) preferred.

  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to “join the dots” and see the bigger picture.

  • Excellent financial modelling, quantitative, and qualitative assessment capabilities.

  • Clear written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders.

  • Highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities.

  • A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with strong integrity and teamwork skills.

jobs.description:

Job Description

Your Team
You will join a global team of 27+ experienced portfolio managers from diverse backgrounds managing a large number of Equity and Fixed Income ETFs. The newly established Taiwan ETF PM team will play an integral part in managing and growing the local ETF business.

About the Role:

  • Manage passive ETFs, index funds, and feeder ETFs in equity, fixed income, or balanced/multi-asset categories.
  • Ensure compliance with Taiwan regulatory requirements and internal investment processes.
  • Oversee index reconstitution, portfolio trading, cash management, and corporate action decisions.
  • Monitor tracking error and implement strategies to optimize portfolio performance.
  • Collaborate with equity and fixed income analysts and traders to implement portfolio decisions.
  • Lead or contribute to investment process improvements to enhance efficiency and scalability.
  • Engage with internal stakeholders (capital markets, product development) and external clients (institutional due diligence, conferences).
  • Utilize advanced portfolio analytics and modeling tools (e.g., Aladdin, Bloomberg, FactSet, Barra/Axioma) for portfolio optimization.
  • Maintain thorough documentation of policies, procedures, and investment decisions.

Requirement for the Role :

  • Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field preferred.
  • CFA designation or progress towards CFA is preferred but not mandatory.
  • 5 to 15 years of asset management experience, specifically managing passive ETFs or mutual funds in both equity and fixed income.
  • Minimum 5 years of direct experience managing or co-managing passive funds/ETFs.
  • Strong knowledge of global equity and fixed income markets, index construction, and passive investment strategies.
  • Experience with portfolio optimization, tracking error management, and corporate action analysis.
  • Excellent communication skills for client engagement and cross-team collaboration.
  • Experience with portfolio management systems such as Aladdin, Bloomberg, or Charles River is preferred.
  • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, SQL/Oracle, and VBA is a significant advantage.

  • On-site presence in Taipei office is mandatory.

  • Ability to work under regulatory requirements, personally implementing investment processes.

    Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment with portfolio managers, analysts, traders, compliance, and operations.

    Speak fluent English to communicate with the global team

  • Certified Securities Investment Analyst (CSIA) License

jobs.title: Portfolio Manager, Agency MBS
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $44 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT.

The Portfolio Manager will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning Agency MBS and related investments across Invesco-managed products.

Responsibilities:

  • Apply Agency MBS expertise to generate and implement investment ideas through comprehensive analysis of specified pools, CMOs, TBAs, and other agency mortgage derivatives

  • Manage portfolios focused primarily on Agency MBS across various investment vehicles, including mutual funds, ETFs and separately managed institutional accounts

  • Collaborate with portfolio management colleagues on investment strategies managed by the broader Structured Investments and Invesco Fixed Income team

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of structured products and other investment grade credit sectors

  • Add value through design and execution of advanced hedging strategies

  • Develop and communicate investment outlook to internal and external stakeholders

  • Build trusted relationships with sell-side counterparties to facilitate best execution on Agency MBS sourcing and trading

  • Cultivate strong, lasting partnerships with current and prospective retail and institutional clients

  • Commercialize Agency MBS and broader structured products investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 10+ years of direct experience in analyzing, trading, and managing risk in Agency MBS including TBA, specified pools, CMOs, and derivatives

  • CFA or MBA preferred

  • Comprehensive understanding of all facets of Agency MBS and related macroeconomic, financial market, and regulatory dynamics

  • Significant knowledge of other structured products sectors

  • Expertise in design and implementation of hedging strategies via swaps, futures, swaptions, and other interest rate derivatives

  • Experience managing levered portfolios is beneficial

  • Command of industry tools including Bloomberg, Tradeweb, Yield Book, DataQuery, etc.

  • Familiarity with BlackRock Aladdin platform is beneficial

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Demonstrated success in driving investment outcomes through decisive risk-taking and portfolio management

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Proficiency leveraging AI tools to automate and optimize workflows

#LI-MTIVZ

Employee

Yes

This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Portfolio Manager will work closely with the Senior Portfolio Manager, Global Equities, to help shape the strategy, direction, and effective management of Global Equities portfolios within Invesco Fundamental Equities’ investment and risk framework. This role requires strong fiduciary responsibility to clients, active engagement with the Global Equities team, and the ability to interact confidently with clients, consultants, brokers, and other external stakeholders. Covers equity securities of companies with market capitalizations of over USD $10B that are headquartered in many developed and emerging countries across the world. Performs due diligence on investments in the portfolio (e.g., conducts site visits, meets with management). Works with the trading desk for securities market trading and provides instructions to traders. May participate in client marketing activities. This position includes direct stock coverage responsibilities and is a part of a team that manages a total of $18.3B as of January 31st, 2026.

Responsibilities of the Role:

Portfolio Management & Investment Process

  • Partner with the Senior Portfolio Manager to develop, refine, and implement strategies for Global Equities portfolios
  • Analyze portfolio composition, performance, and risk attributes to support optimal portfolio outcomes
  • Maintain financial models and active coverage on approximately 20 companies across at least three sectors.
  • Contribute to portfolio construction and integrate quantitative signals in collaboration with internal partners (e.g., quantitative analyst)
  • Review and analyze investment results, attribution, and trends to enhance performance with strong fiduciary discipline
  • Work with the Senior Portfolio Manager to develop and maintain an effective investment process, including trading policies, procedures, and annual business goals

Research & Market Coverage

  • Monitor legislative, regulatory, and macroeconomic developments affecting global equities
  • Interface with the ECM desk regarding potential IPO opportunities
  • Collaborate with ESG and Global Investment Forum teams 

Risk, Compliance & Governance

  • Ensure full compliance with regulatory requirements and internal risk frameworks
  • Engage regularly with the Risk team to review portfolio exposures, discuss findings, and provide feedback
  • Prepare materials for investment committees and respond to ad hoc information requests from senior management

Client, Consultant & Internal Partner Engagement

  • Represent the Global Equities team to internal and external stakeholders, including clients, prospects, consultants, brokers, and gatekeepers
  • Market the team’s capabilities to existing and potential clients as well as internal distribution partners
  • Participate in meetings with major wire houses, key accounts, and investment platforms
  • Provide timely information, reporting, and insights to internal partners and external constituencies

Requirements of the role:

  • A Master’s degree in finance, economics, mathematics, business, or a related field is ideal
  • 10+ years of experience in equity research and/or portfolio management
  • Strong understanding of both retail and institutional clients
  • Coverage experience across at least three sectors
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with effective presentation ability
  • Demonstrated success as a team player in collaborative, global, and cross-functional environments
  • Effective interpersonal skills with the ability to influence outcomes across teams and organizational levels
  • Confident engaging with management teams, brokers, consultants, and clients
  • Open-minded, flexible, and receptive to diverse viewpoints
  • Exhibits leadership qualities, intellectual curiosity, and a strong work ethic
  • Ability to travel internationally required
  • CFA designation strongly preferred

The salary range for this position in New York is $250,000 to $280,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location

The Senior Associate, Private Markets Product, Private Credit focuses on supporting business development strategy initiatives for private credit business across broadly syndicated loans, CLOs, direct lending and distressed credit & special situations. It involves close collaboration with the private credit team: Portfolio Managers, Client Portfolio Managers, and Product Managers. jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.
